audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Leviticus / Vayiqra priestly ordination narrative text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for ordination of Aaron and sons, washing, garments, anointing, sin/purification offering, burnt offering, ordination ram, blood on ear/thumb/toe, seven-day entrance command.

verses.restored: YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Take Aaron and his sons with him, and garments, anointing oil, bull of sin/misalignment offering, two rams, and basket of unleavened bread.
And assemble all congregation at entrance of tent of meeting.
Moses did as YHWH commanded him, and congregation assembled at entrance of tent of meeting.
Moses said to congregation, This is the matter that YHWH commanded to be done.
Moses brought Aaron and his sons near and washed them with water.
He put tunic on him, girded him with sash, clothed him with robe, put ephod upon him, girded him with woven band of ephod, and bound it to him with it.
He placed breastpiece upon him, and put into breastpiece the Urim and Thummim.
He set turban on his head, and on turban at front he set gold plate, holy crown, as YHWH commanded Moses.
Moses took anointing oil and anointed dwelling-place and all that was in it, and sanctified them.
He sprinkled from it on altar seven times, anointed altar and all its vessels, basin and its stand, to sanctify them.
He poured from anointing oil on Aaron's head and anointed him, to sanctify him.
Moses brought Aaron's sons near, clothed them with tunics, girded them with sashes, and bound caps on them, as YHWH commanded Moses.
He brought near bull of sin/misalignment offering, and Aaron and his sons laid hands on head of bull of sin/misalignment offering.

He slaughtered it, and Moses took blood/life, put it on horns of altar all around with his finger, and purified/cleansed altar; he poured blood/life at base of altar and sanctified it, to make atonement/covering upon it.
He took all fat on inward parts, lobe of liver, two kidneys and their fat, and Moses turned them into smoke on altar.
But bull, its skin, flesh, and dung he burned with fire outside camp, as YHWH commanded Moses.
He brought near ram of burnt offering, and Aaron and his sons laid hands on head of ram.
He slaughtered it, and Moses threw blood/life on altar all around.
The ram he cut into its pieces, and Moses turned head, pieces, and fat into smoke.
Inward parts and legs he washed with water, and Moses turned all ram into smoke on altar. It was burnt offering for soothing scent, fire-offering to YHWH, as YHWH commanded Moses.
He brought near second ram, ram of ordination/filling, and Aaron and his sons laid hands on head of ram.
He slaughtered it, and Moses took from its blood/life and put on lobe of Aaron's right ear, thumb of his right hand, and big toe of his right foot.
He brought Aaron's sons near, and Moses put from blood/life on lobe of their right ear, thumb of their right hand, and big toe of their right foot; Moses threw blood/life on altar all around.
He took fat, fat tail, all fat on inward parts, lobe of liver, two kidneys and their fat, and right thigh.
From basket of unleavened bread that was before YHWH he took one unleavened cake, one cake of oiled bread, and one wafer, and placed them on fat portions and right thigh.
He put all on palms of Aaron and palms of his sons and waved them as wave offering before YHWH.
Moses took them from their palms and turned them into smoke on altar upon burnt offering; they were ordination/filling offerings for soothing scent, fire-offering to YHWH.
Moses took breast and waved it as wave offering before YHWH; from ram of ordination/filling it was Moses' portion, as YHWH commanded Moses.

Moses took from anointing oil and from blood/life that was on altar and sprinkled on Aaron, on his garments, on his sons, and on garments of his sons with him; he sanctified Aaron, his garments, his sons, and garments of his sons with him.

Moses said to Aaron and his sons, Boil flesh at entrance of tent of meeting, and there eat it and bread in basket of ordination/filling offerings, as I commanded, saying, Aaron and his sons shall eat it.
What remains from flesh and bread you shall burn with fire.
From entrance of tent of meeting you shall not go out seven days, until day of completing days of your ordination/filling; for seven days he shall fill your hand.
As he has done this day, YHWH commanded to do, to make atonement/covering for you.
At entrance of tent of meeting you shall stay day and night seven days, and keep watch of YHWH's charge, so you do not die, for so I was commanded.
Aaron and his sons did all matters that YHWH commanded by hand of Moses.

translation_notes: Leviticus 8 narrates performance of Exodus 29 ordination instructions.
Ordination/filling-hand idiom is preserved to show installation into service.
Blood/life on ear, thumb, and toe marks hearing, doing, and walking in consecrated service.
Seven-day staying at tent entrance is ritual formation and boundary, not arbitrary confinement.
safety_note: Priestly ordination is accountable service, not status entitlement. Ritual blood/life symbolism should be handled with solemnity and not sensationalized.
